heading: Emergency use of medical products
label: 360bbb–3a Emergency use of medical products

children.label: A
children.text: is approved or cleared under this subchapter, conditionally approved under , or licensed under section 351 of the Public Health Service Act [];section 360ccc of this title42 U.S.C. 262

children.label: i
children.text: is intended for use to prevent, diagnose, or treat a disease or condition involving a biological, chemical, radiological, or nuclear agent or agents; or

children.label: ii
children.text: is intended for use to prevent, diagnose, or treat a serious or life-threatening disease or condition caused by a product described in clause (i); and

children.label: i
children.text: a determination described in subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) of has been made by the Secretary of Homeland Security, the Secretary of Defense, or the Secretary, respectively; orsection 360bbb–3(b)(1) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the identification of a material threat described in subparagraph (D) of has been made pursuant to section 319F–2 of the Public Health Service Act [].section 360bbb–3(b)(1) of this title42 U.S.C. 247d–6b

children.heading: Product
children.label: 2 Product
children.text: The term “product” means a drug, device, or biological product.

children.label: i
children.text: the public health; or

children.label: ii
children.text: military preparedness and effectiveness; and

children.label: B
children.text: the expiration date extension is supported by an appropriate scientific evaluation that is conducted or accepted by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: each specific lot, batch, or other unit of the product for which extended expiration is authorized;

children.label: B
children.text: the duration of the extension; and

children.label: C
children.text: any other requirements or conditions as the Secretary may deem appropriate for the protection of the public health, which may include requirements for, or conditions on, product sampling, storage, packaging or repackaging, transport, labeling, notice to product recipients, recordkeeping, periodic testing or retesting, or product disposition.

children.heading: Effect
children.label: 3 Effect
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this chapter or the Public Health Service Act [ et seq.], an eligible product shall not be considered an unapproved product (as defined in ) and shall not be deemed adulterated or misbranded under this chapter because, with respect to such product, the Secretary has, under paragraph (1), extended the expiration date and authorized the introduction or delivery for introduction into interstate commerce of such product after the expiration date provided by the manufacturer.42 U.S.C. 201section 360bbb–3(a)(2)(A) of this title

children.heading: Expiration date
children.label: 4 Expiration date
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “expiration date” means the date established through appropriate stability testing required by the regulations issued by the Secretary to ensure that the product meets applicable standards of identity, strength, quality, and purity at the time of use.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may, when the circumstances of a domestic, military, or public health emergency or material threat described in subsection (a)(1)(C) so warrant, authorize, with respect to an eligible product, deviations from current good manufacturing practice requirements otherwise applicable to the manufacture, processing, packing, or holding of products subject to regulation under this chapter, including requirements under section 351 or 360j(f)(1) of this title or applicable conditions prescribed with respect to the eligible product by an order under .section 360j(f)(2) of this title

children.heading: Effect
children.label: 2 Effect
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this chapter or the Public Health Service Act [ et seq.], an eligible product shall not be considered an unapproved product (as defined in ) and shall not be deemed adulterated or misbranded under this chapter because, with respect to such product, the Secretary has authorized deviations from current good manufacturing practices under paragraph (1).42 U.S.C. 201section 360bbb–3(a)(2)(A) of this title
the product is dispensed during the circumstances described in subsection (a)(1)(C); and

children.label: A
children.text: as permitted under the law of the State in which the product is dispensed; or

children.label: B
children.text: in accordance with an order issued by the Secretary, for the purposes and duration of the circumstances described in subsection (a)(1)(C).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary, acting through an appropriate official within the Department of Health and Human Services, may create and issue emergency use instructions to inform health care providers or individuals to whom an eligible product is to be administered concerning such product’s approved, licensed, or cleared conditions of use.

children.label: A
children.text: during an emergency response to an actual emergency that is the basis for a determination described in subsection (a)(1)(C); or

children.label: B
children.text: by a government entity (including a Federal, State, local, or tribal government entity), or a person acting on behalf of such a government entity, in preparation for an emergency response.
